sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) adj. verbal pass. (from the root מָשַׁה), χριστός, anointed (used of a shield), 2Sa 1:21 מָשִׁיחַ נָגִיד the anointed prince [Messiah the prince], Dan 9:25 הַכֹּחֵן הַמָּשִׁיחַ the anointed priest, i.e. the high priest, Lev 4:3, , 16 .

(2) subst. Χριστός, Anointed, i.e. prince (consecrated by anointing), Dan 9:26 more fully מְשִׁיחַ יְיָ the anointed of Jehovah (LXX. ὁ Χριστὸς Κυρίου), a title of honour given to the kings of Israel as being consecrated to God by anointing, and, therefore, holy, 1Sa 2:10, 35 1Sa 2:35 12:3 , 1Sa 12:5 16:6 24:7 , 11 1Sa 24:11 26:9 , 11 1Sa 26:11, 23 1Sa 26:23 2Sa 1:14, 16 2Sa 1:16 19:22 23:1 Psa 2:2 18:51 20:7 28:8 . Once used of Cyrus, king of Persia, Isa 45:1 never of the future Messiah (although some refer Psa 2:2 to him). [This is an awfully false statement, many of these passages (as well as Psa 2:2) refer to Christ only.] Plur. the anointed of Jehovah, of the Patriarchs, Psa 105:15 1Ch 16:22.
